name: Asparagus Salad with Pecans
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to boil.
2. Wash the asparagus and snap off the tough bottoms of the stems.
3. When the water is boiling, add the asparagus and cook until tender but crisp, about 3 minutes.
4. Remove the asparagus with tongs and run it under cold water to stop cooking.
5. Chill the asparagus in the refrigerator.
6. At serving time, place lettuce on six salad plates.
7. Arrange four asparagus spears on each plate.
8. Drizzle each salad with 1 tablespoon of buttermilk mayonnaise or commercial light mayonnaise.
9. Sprinkle chopped pecans over the salads.
Food Exchange per Serving: 1 VEGETABLE EXCHANGE; CHO: 9g; PRO: 3g; FAT: 2g; CAL: 41
Low-sodium diets: This recipe is excellent.
Source: The Art of Cooking for the Diabetic by Mary Abbott Hess, R.D., M.S. and Katharine Middleton
